steps: Triangle \(QRS\) has a right angle at \(R\). The side \(SR = 47\) is the side opposite angle \(Q\). Since the right angle is at \(R\), the hypotenuse is \(QS\). The condition \(QR < QS\) confirms \(QS\) is the hypotenuse.
Apply the sine ratio from angle \(Q\): \(\sin Q = \dfrac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \dfrac{SR}{QS} = \dfrac{47}{QS}\).
Solve for \(QS\): multiply both sides by \(QS\) and divide by \(\sin Q\) to get \(QS = \dfrac{47}{\sin Q}\).

• Choice A: \(47\cos Q\) — cosine times a leg gives a shorter segment, not the hypotenuse; incorrect.
  • Choice B: \(47\sin Q\) — multiplying by sine shrinks the value below 47, but \(QS\) is the hypotenuse and must be the longest side; incorrect.
  • Choice C: \(\dfrac{47}{\cos Q}\) — this would follow from \(\cos Q = 47/QS\), meaning 47 is the adjacent side; but 47 = SR is opposite to \(Q\), not adjacent; incorrect.
  • Choice D: \(\dfrac{47}{\sin Q}\) — since \(\sin Q = 47/QS\), solving gives \(QS = 47/\sin Q\). That matches choice D — D is correct.
